# Three-scatter — Full Documentation

> Scatter meshes and 3D models across any Three.js geometry. Inspired by GScatter for Blender.

Package: `@jaimebboyjt/three-scatter`
Repository: https://github.com/JaimeTorrealba/three-scatter

---

## Introduction

`ThreeScatter` extends `THREE.Group`. It samples random points on the faces of a `BufferGeometry` and places clones of one or more meshes at those points.

### When to use three-scatter

Three.js ships `MeshSurfaceSampler` for high-performance instanced sampling. Use `three-scatter` instead when you need:

- Complex 3D models (animations, rigging, skinning)
- Surface-normal alignment per object
- Real-time seed tweaking (e.g. leva / dat.GUI panels)
- Automatic collision removal
- Axis-filtered visibility
- Per-model distribution weights

> If raw performance is the priority, prefer `MeshSurfaceSampler` with `InstancedMesh`.

### Installation

```bash
npm i @jaimebboyjt/three-scatter
# or
yarn add @jaimebboyjt/three-scatter
# or
pnpm add @jaimebboyjt/three-scatter
```

### Basic Usage

```js
import { ThreeScatter } from '@jaimebboyjt/three-scatter'

const scatter = new ThreeScatter(count, surface, model);
scene.add(scatter);
```

---

## API Reference

### Constructor

```ts
new ThreeScatter(count: number, base: BufferGeometry, mesh?: Mesh | Mesh[], options?: Options)
```

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `count` | `number` | Number of objects to scatter. |
| `base` | `BufferGeometry` | Surface geometry to scatter on. |
| `mesh` | `Mesh \| Mesh[]` | Object or array of objects to scatter. Omit for take-over mode. |
| `options` | `Options` | Optional configuration (see below). |

### Options interface

```ts
interface Options {
  seeds?: number          // default: 1
  useSkeletonUtils?: boolean  // default: false
  distribution?: number[]     // default: undefined
  debug?: boolean             // default: false
  debugGeometry?: BufferGeometry  // default: SphereGeometry(0.5, 3, 2)
  debugMaterial?: Material        // default: MeshBasicMaterial({ color: 0x800080 })
}
```

| Property | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `seeds` | `number` | `1` | PRNG seed for reproducible layouts. |
| `useSkeletonUtils` | `boolean` | `false` | Use `SkeletonUtils.clone` — required for rigged/animated models. |
| `distribution` | `number[]` | `undefined` | Per-model probability weights. Must match mesh array length and sum to `1`. |
| `debug` | `boolean` | `false` | Enable debug proxy mode at construction. |
| `debugGeometry` | `BufferGeometry` | `SphereGeometry(0.5,3,2)` | Geometry for debug markers. |
| `debugMaterial` | `Material` | `MeshBasicMaterial({color:0x800080})` | Material for debug markers. |

### Methods

#### `setSeeds(seed?)`
```ts
setSeeds(seed?: number): void
```
Re-seeds the PRNG and repositions all scattered objects. Useful for real-time tweaking.

#### `setAll(callback)`
```ts
setAll(callback: (model: Object3D, index: number) => void): void
```
Iterates every scattered child. Use it to apply per-object scale, rotation, or custom logic.

#### `setAxis(x, y, z)`
```ts
setAxis(
  x: [min: number, max: number],
  y: [min: number, max: number],
  z: [min: number, max: number]
): void
```
Shows or hides objects based on the surface normal at their position. Ranges are normalized between `-1` and `1`.

#### `alignToSurfaceNormal()`
```ts
alignToSurfaceNormal(): void
```
Rotates each object so its `Y+` axis aligns with the face normal of the triangle it was placed on.

#### `removeCollisions()`
```ts
removeCollisions(): void
```
Computes AABBs for all children and removes any that intersect another.

#### `setDebug()`
```ts
setDebug(): void
```
Hides all scattered objects and shows an `InstancedMesh` of low-poly proxies.

#### `removeDebug()`
```ts
removeDebug(): void
```
Restores scattered object visibility and hides the debug proxy mesh.

#### `getFaces()`
```ts
getFaces(): Triangle[]
```
Returns all `THREE.Triangle` faces extracted from the surface geometry.

#### `getPositions()`
```ts
getPositions(): Vector3[]
```
Returns all sampled `THREE.Vector3` positions. Useful in take-over mode.

#### `cleanGroup()`
```ts
cleanGroup(): void
```
Disposes geometry and materials of every child mesh, then clears the group.

---

## Examples

### Multiple objects

```js
import { ThreeScatter } from '@jaimebboyjt/three-scatter'

const scatter = new ThreeScatter(count, surface, [model1, model2]);
scene.add(scatter);
```

### Seeds

```js
const scatter = new ThreeScatter(count, surface, model, { seeds: 1 });

// Change at any time:
scatter.setSeeds(5);
```

### Align to surface normal

```js
const scatter = new ThreeScatter(count, surface, model);
scatter.alignToSurfaceNormal();
```

### Remove collisions

```js
const scatter = new ThreeScatter(count, surface, model);
scatter.removeCollisions();
```

### Display by axis

```js
// Show only objects on the upper half of the Y axis:
scatter.setAxis([-1, 1], [0, 1], [-1, 1]);
```

### Take-over mode (positions only)

```js
const scatter = new ThreeScatter(count, surface);
const positions = scatter.getPositions();

positions.forEach((pos) => {
  const mesh = new THREE.Mesh(geometry, material);
  mesh.position.copy(pos);
  scene.add(mesh);
});
```

### Debug mode

```js
import * as THREE from 'three'

const scatter = new ThreeScatter(count, surface, model, {
  debug: true,
  debugGeometry: new THREE.SphereGeometry(0.5, 3, 2),
  debugMaterial: new THREE.MeshBasicMaterial({ color: 0x800080 }),
});

// Toggle after construction:
scatter.setDebug();
scatter.removeDebug();
```

### Distribution (multi-model)

```js
// 25% model1, 75% model2
const scatter = new ThreeScatter(count, surface, [model1, model2], {
  distribution: [0.25, 0.75],
});
```

### Animated / rigged models

```js
const scatter = new ThreeScatter(count, surface, animatedModel, {
  useSkeletonUtils: true,
});
```

### Per-object transforms

```js
scatter.setAll((model, index) => {
  const s = 0.5 + Math.random();
  model.scale.set(s, s, s);
});
```

### Dispose

```js
scatter.cleanGroup();
```

---

## Notes

- `ThreeScatter` extends `THREE.Group`, so all standard `Object3D` / `Group` methods (`.position`, `.scale`, `.rotation`, `.add`, `.remove`, etc.) are available on the instance.
- `distribution` requires an array of equal length to the `mesh` array with values summing to exactly `1`.
- `useSkeletonUtils` must be `true` whenever scattering models that contain a `SkinnedMesh` or skeletal animations.
- In take-over mode (no `mesh` argument), none of the object-management methods (`alignToSurfaceNormal`, `removeCollisions`, `setDebug`, etc.) will have any effect since the group has no children.
